title: Roundtable opening interpretation
description: One model seat's independent, blinded interpretation of the user's prompt. The seat only determines what is being asked; it does not solve, plan, or implement.

restated_request.description: A plain-language restatement of what the user is asking for, in the seat's own words.

intent.description: The core goals or outcomes the user appears to want.

scope_in.description: What the seat believes is included in the request.

scope_out.description: What the seat believes is explicitly NOT being asked for.

assumptions.description: Assumptions the seat is making to arrive at this interpretation.

ambiguities.description: Points in the request that could reasonably be read more than one way.

terminology.description: Key domain terms the interpretation relies on, in the project's canonical vocabulary, flagging any conflict with the glossary (CONTEXT.md).

meaning.description: The meaning the seat assigns it for this request.

conflicts_glossary.description: True if this meaning conflicts with the existing glossary definition.

code_findings.description: Contradictions or confirmations the seat found by checking its reading against the actual code (read-only). Empty if no code was consulted.

open_questions.description: Questions whose answers would change this interpretation.

confidence.description: The seat's confidence (0-100) that its interpretation matches the user's intent.
